answer: Oh, sure, I remember this one interview I had—it was actually for a part-time job at a local coffee shop. The manager, who seemed really friendly but, you know, kind of professional, was the one conducting it. To prepare, I kind of went over what I’d say if they asked me about my strengths or why I wanted the job. I also looked into the company a bit just to sound like, um, I cared about their values. Honestly, I tried practicing answering typical questions with my parents, so I wouldn’t get too nervous. During the interview, it felt like a conversation more than anything, which helped me relax. Overall, it wasn’t as intimidating as I had expected, and I think being prepared gave me a lot of confidence!
title:
Describe an interview you had, either for a job or another activity.
What was the interview for?
Who conducted the interview?
How did you prepare?
